meta.label: Jira wiki agenda
meta.description: Pull Jira issues by filter, compare with latest work-week Confluence child page via LLM, create a new child page with change report and output a copy of it.

outputRoles.name: main_output
outputRoles.label: New child page (copy)
outputRoles.description: Dataset with one row: the newly created Confluence child page (page_id, title, body_storage, url, space_key, version_number, created_at, parent_page_id).

params.name: jira_token
params.label: Jira token
params.description: Jira API token (Bearer or Basic password)

params.name: jira_filter_id
params.label: Jira filter ID
params.description: Filter id used in JQL: filter=<id>

params.name: jira_fields
params.label: Jira fields to fetch
params.description: Fields to request from the Jira search API. Each row: display title (shown to the LLM) and API field id (e.g. key, summary, customfield_12345).

params.name: wiki_base_url
params.label: Wiki base URL
params.description: Confluence base URL, e.g. https://wiki.example.com (no trailing slash)

params.name: wiki_parent_page_id
params.label: Wiki parent page ID
params.description: Confluence content id of the parent page under which work-week child pages live

params.name: llm_selection
params.label: LLM
params.description: Dataiku LLM to use for comparing Jira state vs previous page and generating the change report

params.name: llm_system_prompt
params.label: LLM system prompt
params.description: Instructions for the LLM: compose a status report from the full Jira issue data and compare it to the previous week's written report (narrative comparison, not value-only diffs).
params.defaultValue: You are a technical writer. You will receive full current Jira issue data and, when available, the previous week's Confluence report. Use this exact top-level section order: (1) ## Executive Summary (2) ## Change Summary (3) ## Issue Status Summaries. In Change Summary, include a markdown table with this exact header: | Issue | Assignee | Status | Change | Comments/Updates | and one row per issue from the data. For Change, use only these emojis: ✅ closed/resolved; 📈 increasing or high escalation, still progressing but not near closure; 📉 decreasing or low priority, progressing toward closure; ⚠️ stalled, no updates, customer concerned about pace; 🚨 blocker/escalation/major issue. In Issue Status Summaries, determine the most significant issues directly from the Jira data, include all high priority/exposure tickets (P1/P2 where present), and provide line-by-line entries for those issues. In Comments/Updates, use plain language: what is happening, why, what is next, and blockers. Compare against previous week when provided. Use markdown headings (##, ###), not bold section titles. Output raw markdown only; do not wrap in code fences.
